scenario_name: Scenario 11 — Queue Backpressure and Consumer Lag
justification: All pass criteria met: throughput gap clearly identified (300/min vs 15-20/min), unbounded queue growth described, visibility timeout double-processing risk identified with arithmetic proof (10×3s=30s=timeout), missing DLQ flagged as critical blocker, queue depth metric gap explicitly called out, autoscaling recommended (ceil(300/17)≈18 instances), verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Throughput gap calculated precisely with backlog growth rate (~280 msgs/min) and 1-hour projection (16,000 messages)
Visibility timeout risk explained as near-certainty rather than edge case, with clear arithmetic
DLQ absence framed as critical blocker with infinite retry and cost implications
Queue depth metric gap called out as the primary undetectable failure mode
Autoscaling recommendation includes concrete sizing (15-20 instances) and scaling metric (ApproximateNumberOfMessagesVisible)
Goes beyond rubric requirements with additional valuable findings: circuit breaker absence, memory exhaustion risk, idempotency concerns, rollback challenges
Escalations are well-structured and clearly explain why each issue is a blocker rather than a nice-to-have
